name: Marcus Webb
content: Hi — Marcus Webb from Cascadia Freight. Half my team can't log into FleetView since this morning. We use SSO through Okta.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Thanks Marcus. Are the affected users seeing an error from FleetView itself, or does the Okta step fail before FleetView loads?

name: Marcus Webb
content: Okta works — they land on FleetView and then get 'no fleet assigned to this account'.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: That means the SSO group mapping changed: FleetView assigns fleets by Okta group, and users outside a mapped group get that message. Did your IT team rename or restructure Okta groups recently?

name: Marcus Webb
content: Yes — they consolidated groups over the weekend. The old 'cascadia-ops' group is now 'cascadia-operations'.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: I've updated the FleetView mapping for Cascadia Freight to the new 'cascadia-operations' group. Affected users should sign out and back in; access is restored on the next login.

name: Marcus Webb
content: Confirmed, everyone is back in. Thanks for the fast turnaround.

name: Marcus Webb
content: Different issue: one of our PickPoint One arms at the Tacoma site keeps failing pick attempts on the smallest bin. Started after yesterday's maintenance.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: After maintenance, the usual cause is a skipped gripper calibration. In FleetView, open the arm's detail page — does the last calibration timestamp predate the maintenance window?

name: Marcus Webb
content: It does — last calibration shows three days ago, maintenance was yesterday.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Run the guided calibration from the arm's service panel: Service > Calibrate Gripper, about six minutes end to end. The crew that did the maintenance can run it; no Meridian visit needed.

name: Marcus Webb
content: Calibration done and the arm has cleared forty picks on the small bin without a fault. Calling it resolved.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Great. I've noted the missed post-maintenance calibration on the Tacoma arm's record so the pattern is visible if it recurs.

name: Marcus Webb
content: We're adding two new shift leads at Cascadia. What's the right way to get them productive on FleetView?

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Add them to your 'cascadia-operations' Okta group for access, then have them start with the Shift Lead dashboard — pick rates and fault trends are the two panels shift leads use hourly.

name: Marcus Webb
content: Is there a sandbox, or do they learn on live data?

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Live data, read-only by default — new FleetView accounts can't acknowledge faults or change lane assignments until you grant the operator role, so there's nothing they can break while learning.

name: Marcus Webb
content: Perfect, that's how I'll run it. Thanks.
